1.title: Sum of Two Numbers
1.description:
Given two integers a and b, return their sum.

You must implement the function returning the combined value of both integers.
constraints: All inputs fit inside standard integer limits.

2.title: Reverse a String
2.description:
Given a string str, return it reversed.

For example, if the input is "hello", the output should be "olleh".

examples.explanation: Characters are reversed from end to start.

constraints: String contains only printable ASCII characters.

3.title: Find Maximum Number
3.description: Given an array of numbers arr, find and return the maximum number in that array.

examples.explanation: 5 is the largest number in the array.

4.title: Check Palindrome
4.description: Given a string str, check if it reads the same forward and backward. Return true if it is a palindrome, otherwise return false.

5.title: Count Vowels
5.description: Given a string str, count and return the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u) present in the string (case-insensitive).

6.title: Fibonacci Series
6.description: Given an integer n, return the n-th Fibonacci number. The series starts with F(0) = 0 and F(1) = 1, where F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2).

examples.explanation: F(5) is computed as F(4) + F(3) = 3 + 2 = 5.

7.title: Prime Number Checker
7.description: Given an integer n, check if it is a prime number. Return true if it is prime, otherwise return false.

examples.explanation: 7 is only divisible by 1 and itself.

8.title: Missing Number in Array
8.description: Given an array arr containing n - 1 distinct numbers selected from the range 1 to n, find and return the one missing number.

examples.explanation: The number 3 is missing from range 1-4.
The number 7 is missing from range 1-8.

constraints: All values in array are distinct and between 1 and n.

9.title: Rotate Array
9.description: Given an array nums and a non-negative integer k, rotate the array to the right by k steps. Return the rotated array.

10.title: Longest Substring
10.description: Given a string str, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

examples.explanation: The longest substring is "abc", with a length of 3.
The longest substring is "b", with a length of 1.

constraints: String contains English letters, digits, symbols and spaces.

11.title: Longest Common Prefix
11.description: Write a function to find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings strs. If there is no common prefix, return an empty string "".

examples.explanation: "fl" is the common prefix of all three strings.

12.title: Valid Parentheses
12.description: Given a string str containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. Return true if valid, otherwise return false.

examples.explanation: Brackets open and close in correct sequence.
Parentheses close with non-matching square bracket.

13.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
13.description: Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays. The output must be a float value.

14.title: Edit Distance
14.description: Given two strings word1 and word2, return the minimum number of operations required to convert word1 to word2. You have three operations: Insert, Delete, and Replace a character.

15.title: Trapping Rain Water
15.description: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

17.title: Middle of the Linked List
17.description:
Given the head of a singly linked list, return the middle node of the linked list.

If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node.

examples.explanation: The middle node of the list is node 3.

constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [1, 100].

18.title: Binary Tree Preorder Traversal
18.description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the preorder traversal of its nodes' values.

examples.explanation: Preorder traversal is root -> left -> right.

constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100].

19.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
19.description: Merge two sorted linked lists and return it as a sorted list.
constraints: The number of nodes in both lists is in the range [0, 50].

20.title: Invert Binary Tree
20.description: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree, and return its root.
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100].

21.title: Valid Parentheses
21.description: Given a string s containing just characters, determine if the input string is valid.

22.title: Binary Search
22.description: Given an array of integers nums which is sorted in ascending order, search for target.

23.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List
23.description: Given the head of a sorted linked list, delete all duplicates such that each element appears only once.
constraints: The list is guaranteed to be sorted in ascending order.

24.title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
24.description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth.
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 10^4].

25.title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
25.description: Find two numbers such that they add up to a specific target number, returning their 1-based indices.

26.title: Search in a Binary Search Tree
26.description: Find the node in the BST that the node's value equals val and return the subtree rooted with that node.
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [1, 5000].

27.title: Map Word Frequencies
27.description: Given a space-separated string of words, count the frequency of each word and return it as a key-value Map.
constraints: Word strings fit in standard memory space.

28.title: JSON Parser Validator
28.description: Given a JSON object representing a user, validate that it contains a non-empty string name and an age >= 18.
constraints: Inputs are valid parsed JSON structures.

29.title: Console I/O: Sum of List Elements
29.description: Write a complete program that reads space-separated integers from Standard Input (stdin) and prints their combined sum to Standard Output (stdout).
constraints: Inputs separated by standard space character.

templates.javascript: // Write your code here
templates.python: # Write your code here

31.title: Group Values by Key
31.description: Given a JSON array of objects containing a group string and a val string, group values by keys, separating values by commas.

32.title: Console I/O: Find Prime Numbers
32.description: Read a single integer N from stdin, and print all prime numbers up to N separated by spaces.

templates.javascript: // Write your code here
templates.python: # Write your code here

33.title: Nested JSON Value Extract
33.description: Given a JSON document and a path string (like user.info.city), extract the string value.
constraints: Inputs are valid parsed JSON documents.

34.title: Console I/O: Anagram Check
34.description: Read two words from separate lines of stdin, and print YES if they are anagrams, otherwise print NO.

templates.javascript: // Write your code here
templates.python: # Write your code here

35.title: Rotate Matrix
35.description: Given a 2D matrix representing an image, rotate the image by 90 degrees (clockwise).
constraints: The matrix dimensions are N x N.

36.title: Console I/O: Fibonacci Sequence Stream
36.description: Read a single integer N from stdin, and print the first N Fibonacci numbers separated by a space.

templates.javascript: // Write your code here
